sections.title: Starting out
Talking to the zealot again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Hello again. I'm afraid I'm rather preoccupied. What was it you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you explain the history of these mines again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there any other way into the mines?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Indeed I have found hope that this may be so. By making discreet inquiries I have been led to believe that a second entrance may exist into the mines.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Further, I have come into possession of a key that will apparently afford entrance to the lower levels of the mines once inside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the second entrance to the mines?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Of this I am not yet sure. From what I have been able to gather, it was not originally built for humans to enter by. Where it may be, I am still endeavoring to find out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can you be discreet with THAT star emblazoned on your robes?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Saradomin watches over me, I am not troubled by those who are not meant to notice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Even so, you're not exactly keeping quiet about your allegiance are you?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Saradomin will protect me. Even if I should fall there are many behind me who would stand in my place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that much consolation when you get chopped up into little pieces?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I would rather be cut into little pieces whilst proclaiming the goodness of Saradomin than hiding my faith.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't see anyone behind you.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Although you may not realise it, Saradomin works quietly in many people, such is the folly of looking with your eyes and not with your heart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I would be one of them. So, can I borrow that key?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I am afraid my task can be entrusted to nobody else. Saradomin has asked me alone to do this. I cannot take the slightest risk of losing this key. The stakes are too high.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right, I was wrong to doubt Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Let not your faith in Saradomin falter, for all else is insignificant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're nuts, goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I borrow your key?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I am afraid my task can be entrusted to nobody else. Saradomin has asked me alone to do this. I cannot take the slightest risk of losing this key. The stakes are too high.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please? I'll be very careful with it.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: You know not what you deal with. There is evil in that mine and, whatever your assurances, I cannot risk you falling to the creatures within.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you think you'll be safer in the mine than me?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Of course I'll be safe, my faith in Saradomin protects me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll trade for it.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Saradomin provides for my every need. There is nothing you could offer me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pretty pretty please?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Saradomin grant me patience. The answer is no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't want that key anyway.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Witness the goodness of Saradomin, that he has granted you the wisdom to see our positions in perspective.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does a lost artefact that was only legendary help?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Although the amulet was lost, it is my hope that if I can make it into the mines I can craft another.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why was everyone in the mines slaughtered? Surely it wasn't their fault that the crystals formed?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: It is said that some of the workers in the mines, merely from being in the presence of these crystals, found themselves bestowed with the love of Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: The advantage to our cause was considered too great by some within Morytania. They set about mercilessly and brutally killing everyone in the mines rather than risk losing them to us.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Such is the treachery of the servants of evil, that they even destroy their own kind without a moments thought.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: The mines are now rumoured to be haunted by the souls of all those brutally and needlessly killed.

sections.title: Talking to the zealot after finding the second entrance

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Hello again. I'm afraid I'm rather preoccupied. What was it you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found the second entrance to the mine, I've managed to make it inside.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: You are foolhardy indeed should you venture inside. The presence of evil is strong within the mines. If you take the risk I shall pray for your safety, although I hold little hope for your safe return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Unfortunately I need a key to use the lift that goes to the lower levels. Can you help?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Indeed? My own inquiries have led me into possession of a key. I was informed that this key would apparently afford entrance to the lower levels. It therefore seems likely that this is the key which you seek.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that sounds like the one. May I borrow it?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I am afraid my task can be entrusted to nobody else. Saradomin has asked me alone to do this. I cannot take the slightest risk of losing this key. The stakes are too high.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've made it in to the mines. Unfortunately some parts are too dark to navigate.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Ah ye of little faith. Let Saradomin guide you even in the darkest places. By his light you may reach your goal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As much as I respect your fanaticism, that was spectacularly unhelpful.

sections.title: Talking to the zealot after encountering Treus Dayth

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: G... g... g... ghost! Scary ghost! Machinery flying everywhere.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I warned you not to enter those mines. It sounds like you've met Dayth. You must not go back down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who did you say Dayth was again?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Treus Dayth was Zamorak's mage in charge of defending the ridge during the time of the orginal amulet.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Dayth failed and fell during an incursion by Saradominists, who then made it into the mines and were able to remove a shard of crystal.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Zamorak, furious at his mage, condemned Dayth's soul indefinitely to an existence of turmoil in the mines. Dayth's soul is bound to prevent the crystals from ever being reached again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nonsense, he'll be no match for me.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I don't know if you think your persistance makes you heroic, but you must realise it's just going to get you killed.

sections.title: Talking to the zealot after killing Treus Dayth

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I met that Dayth character you talked about. He wasn't as tough as all that.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I was afraid this would happen. The fear you must have experienced within those mines has left you delusional. There's no way you could have survived had you actually fought Dayth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: State thy allegiance stranger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I follow the path of Saradomin. Strength through wisdom.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Ah, a wise choice my friend. But if you truly follow Saradomin, what brings you to these evil lands?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I come seeking challenges and quests.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: A noble cause indeed. One that I can sympathise with, for I myself am upon a quest. One that will allow Saradomin to be glorified even in these dark lands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What quest is that then?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Well, below the very ground you stand upon lies an intricate network of tunnels that make up the abandoned Mort Ridge mines. These mines used to be a valuable resource to the inhabitants of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: As with many things, this changed when Misthalin was finally able to push the minions of Morytania back, and Saradomin himself blessed the river Salve.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: The mines are also the site where the legendary artefact, the salve amulet, was thought to have been carved from the crystals that grow in the lower levels of the mine.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: These crystals are thought to be formed by the blessed water of the river Salve as it percolates through the rocks to the lower levels of the mine.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: It is not known what happened to the salve amulet, but it is generally believed that by encapsulating within it some of the goodness of Saradomin himself, it afforded the bearer power over evil.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Saradomin has entrusted me alone with the task of regaining access to the mines, whatever the dangers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can an abandoned mine be dangerous?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Because the mines are said to be haunted. The crystals and the ridge used to be protected by Zamorak's mage, Treus Dayth.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Dayth failed and fell during an incursion by Saradominists, who then made it into the mines and were able to remove a shard of crystal.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Zamorak, furious at his mage, condemned Dayth's soul indefinitely to an existence of turmoil in the mines. Dayth's soul is bound to prevent the crystals from ever being reached again.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Further, Zamorak's then ruler upon the land ordered the slaughter of anyone within the mines and that the mines be sealed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must be going now, good luck on your quest.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: You should believe not in luck, but in the protection of Saradomin. Farewell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope it goes well. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Goodbye, and may Saradomin give you strength through wisdom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok I lied, I don't support Saradomin. I'm only here for dastardly evil purposes.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Then leave me, I have no time to listen to your poisonous tongue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could ask the same of you.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Saradomin has granted you wisdom. The evil creatures of this land are cunning and you are right to question anyone's purpose here.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I am upon a quest. One that will allow Saradomin to be glorified even in these dark lands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I serve only Zamorak. Strength through chaos.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Then begone. Normally I would strike you down in an instant, but for now I have no time. I am on a quest of the utmost importance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this quest of which you speak?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: It is no concern of yours, but rest assured it will help in crushing you and those others who serve your vile master.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I doubt that.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I would expect nothing less of you, misguided as you already are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, I didn't want to talk to you either.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Fear not fiend, even though I may leave you be, you shall not escape justice at the hands of the righteous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My beliefs are in accord with the teachings of Guthix. Balance is power.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: A wishy washy set of beliefs. You should follow Saradomin and find a real purpose in your life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what sort of purpose would that be?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: To further the glory of Saradomin of course.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Take myself, I am upon a quest that will allow Saradomin to be glorified even in these dark lands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can make my own choices, who are you to dispute them?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I look only to help those who are misguided.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have no particular allegiance.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: Then your life is without reason or direction. I would talk to you further on the matter, but for now my attentions must be elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I shall leave you to your business.

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I shall pray that Saradomin grants you the wisdom to see through your folly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Zealot
lines.text: I am upon a quest. One that will allow Saradomin to be glorified even in these dark lands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's kind of dark down here. I should probably find a light source before going further.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey - my light source isn't working! I'd better find a more suitable one before trying to go any further.

lines.text: What the... ?
